profile.name: Garv Anand
philosophy: I treat every ML model as a product — if it can't survive noisy inputs, edge cases, and production latency, it doesn't ship.
I believe the best AI systems are invisible to their users. ElderAI doesn't ask elderly people to learn technology; it meets them where they are — their voice.
I optimize for learning velocity over stack loyalty. PyTorch today, a new framework tomorrow — the fundamentals of linear algebra, probability, and systems thinking don't change.

experience.focus: Production PDF layout detection & document intelligence pipelines
experience.impact: Built end-to-end vision-parsing pipelines processing 40+ page PDFs with complex table layouts at production scale
details: Engineered YOLOv8-based table detection pipeline for structured data extraction from noisy, variable-layout PDFs
Integrated Microsoft Table Transformer for precise cell-level parsing of detected table regions
Built LlamaIndex RAG pipeline for semantic search over extracted document content
Handled real-world edge cases: rotated scans, multi-column layouts, nested tables, low-resolution inputs

experience.impact: 3 workshops delivered to 150+ students, 92% satisfaction rate

projects.title: ElderAI
projects.subtitle: Voice-First AI Companion for the Elderly
projects.problem_statement: Elderly people who cannot navigate smartphone interfaces are locked out of the benefits of AI assistants. Existing solutions assume screen literacy.
projects.my_specific_contribution: Designed and built the full inference pipeline: voice capture → Whisper STT → intent classification → RAG-powered memory retrieval → LLM response generation → TTS output. Implemented the real-time memory layer that remembers user context across sessions.
projects.description: A multimodal AI companion that uses voice as the primary interaction modality — no screens, no typing. Combines speech-to-text, intent parsing, contextual memory (RAG), and natural voice responses to serve users who can't use traditional apps.
projects.signal: I design AI systems around human limitations, not around what's technically convenient.
projects.impact_metric: End-to-end voice AI pipeline with persistent memory across sessions

architectureFlow.detail: Context retrieval from persistent user history

projects.title: Parkinson's Detection
projects.subtitle: Early Detection from Speech via Bi-LSTM
projects.problem_statement: Early-stage Parkinson's disease is difficult to detect clinically. Speech degradation is one of the earliest biomarkers but requires specialized analysis.
projects.my_specific_contribution: Built the Bi-LSTM model architecture from scratch, implemented GAN-based data augmentation to handle the small dataset (195 entries), and achieved 93% accuracy. Authored the research paper submitted to Elsevier.
projects.description: Engineered a Bi-LSTM model for early Parkinson's disease detection from speech patterns, with GAN-based augmentation to overcome limited training data.
projects.signal: I can take a research problem from architecture design to publication-ready results.
projects.impact_metric: 93% accuracy on benchmarked datasets — paper submitted to Elsevier AI in Medicine (Scopus, In Review)

projects.title: Multimodal Speech Emotion Recognition
projects.subtitle: Deep Learning for Virtual Classroom Engagement
projects.problem_statement: Virtual classrooms lack real-time feedback on student engagement. Instructors cannot gauge emotional state from a grid of muted video feeds.
projects.my_specific_contribution: Built the multimodal fusion layer combining acoustic (Bi-LSTM), visual (CNN), and linguistic (Transformer) feature streams. Trained on RAVDESS and CREMA-D datasets with custom preprocessing pipelines.
projects.description: A multimodal deep learning framework that fuses audio, video, and text signals to classify student emotional states in real-time during virtual lectures.
projects.signal: I can architect multi-stream deep learning systems that fuse heterogeneous data modalities.
projects.impact_metric: Three-stream fusion model (Bi-LSTM + CNN + Transformer) trained on RAVDESS & CREMA-D

projects.title: VITAL-AI
projects.subtitle: ML-Powered Health Risk Prediction
projects.problem_statement: Individuals lack proactive health risk assessment — they only visit doctors after symptoms appear. Early warning systems could trigger preventive action.
projects.my_specific_contribution: Built the Random Forest risk prediction engine and designed the Flask API layer. Integrated Google Fit data sync for continuous vitals monitoring. Built at HackByte 3.0 (IIITDM Jabalpur).
projects.description: A health monitoring platform combining wearable data sync with real-time disease risk prediction using Random Forest models and an intuitive dashboard.
projects.signal: I can build end-to-end ML products under hackathon pressure — from data pipeline to deployed UI.
projects.impact_metric: Real-time risk prediction from combined vitals data — built in 36 hours at HackByte 3.0
